---
title: "Greenland Glacier Retreat Susceptibility Application"
subtitle: "A Google Earth Engine App for Cryosphere–Industry Interaction"
author: "Entao Wang, Penghao Gao, XinLei Shi, Yunjie Zhou, Siyu Chen, Ho Man Chan"
institute: "Data Dishwasher CASA0025: Building Spatial Applications with Big Data"
date: "2026/04/25 (updated: `r Sys.Date()`)"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: [default, xaringan-themer.css]
    lib_dir: libs
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
    seal: false
---

```{r setup_packages, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
library(fontawesome)
library(xaringanthemer)
library(xaringanExtra)
library(knitr)
```

```{r setup_theme, include=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
style_mono_light(
  base_color = "#1b3a4b",
  background_color = "#fafafa",
  header_font_google = google_font("Lato"),
  text_font_google   = google_font("Lato"),
  code_font_google   = google_font("Lato"),
  base_font_size = "20px",
  header_h1_font_size = "2.2rem",
  header_h2_font_size = "1.7rem",
  header_h3_font_size = "1.3rem",
  extra_css = list(
    ".remark-slide-content" = list(
      "font-size" = "20px",
      "line-height" = "1.4"
    ),
    ".title-slide" = list(
      "background-image" = "url('images/greenland_ice.webp')",
      "background-size" = "cover",
      "background-position" = "center",
      "background-repeat" = "no-repeat",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
      ),
    ".title-slide a" = list(
      "color" = "white",
      "text-decoration" = "none"
      ),
    ".title-slide a:visited" = list(
      "color" = "white"
      ),
    ".title-slide h1" = list(
      "font-size" = "44px",
      "margin-bottom" = "16px",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
    ),
    ".title-slide h2" = list(
      "font-size" = "26px",
      "margin-top" = "0px",
      "margin-bottom" = "24px",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
    ),
    ".title-slide h3" = list(
      "font-size" = "18px",
      "font-weight" = "normal",
      "margin-bottom" = "10px",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
    )
  )
)
```

```{r setup_layout, include=FALSE}
options(htmltools.dir.version = FALSE)

style_extra_css(css = list(
   ".pull-left-wide" = list("float" = "left", "width" = "60%"),
   ".pull-right-narrow" = list("float" = "right", "width" = "35%"),
   ".pull-left-3" = list("float" = "left", "width" = "30%"),
   ".pull-middle-3" = list("float" = "left", "width" = "30%", "margin-left" = "5%", "margin-right" = "5%"),
   ".pull-right-3" = list("float" = "right", "width" = "30%"),
   ".bottom-note" = list("position" = "absolute","bottom" = "1em","left" = "1em", "right" = "1em"),
   ".clearfix" = list("clear" = "both", "display" = "block")
))
xaringanExtra::use_panelset()
```

class: center, middle, title-slide

# Monitoring Glacier Retreat Susceptibility in Greenland

## A Google Earth Engine App for Cryosphere–Industry Interaction

### Data Dishwasher

### CASA0025: Building Spatial Applications with Big Data

### (updated: `r Sys.Date()`)

---

# Greenland Context

.pull-left-wide[
**Why Greenland?**

- Greenland’s peripheral glaciers are undergoing rapid retreat under Arctic amplification.
- At the same time, mineral exploration and mining accessibility are becoming more important in fragile cryospheric environments.
- This project builds an integrated Earth Engine platform to explore glacier retreat, warming, terrain controls, and mining-related proximity together.
]

.pull-right-narrow[
```{r fig_greenland_context, echo=FALSE, out.width='100%', fig.align='center'}
knitr::include_graphics("images/greenland_mosaic.jpg")
```
<p style="font-size: 0.6em; text-align: center; margin-top: 0;">
Figure 1: Greenland glacier landscape
</p>
]

.clearfix[]

<div style="background: #1b3a4b; color: white; padding: 10px 16px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 0.9em;">
The app is designed as an exploratory platform for identifying glacier retreat susceptibility hotspots rather than proving direct causality.
</div>

---

class: inverse, center, middle

## Interface logic and layer design

---

# Main App Interface

.pull-left-wide[
**Core interface design**

- Dual-map interface for comparing glacier conditions between around 2000 and 2024
- Layer toggles for glacier extent, retreat, susceptibility, warming, terrain, mining, and settlements
- Click-on-map inspector for local glacier-climate summaries
- Output panel for hotspot interpretation and model diagnostics
]

.pull-right-narrow[
```{r fig_interface_layout, echo=FALSE, out.width='100%', fig.align='center'}
knitr::include_graphics("images/greenland_ice_sheet.jpg")
```
<p style="font-size: 0.6em; text-align: center; margin-top: 0;">
Figure 2: Example dashboard layout for Greenland monitoring
</p>
]

.clearfix[]

---

# Layer Controls

.pull-left[
### Cryosphere layers

- Glacier extent around 2000
- Glacier extent around 2024
- Retreat mask
- Susceptibility surface
]

.pull-right[
### Context layers

- Mining drillholes
- Mineral licence polygons
- Settlements and cities
- ArcticDEM elevation and slope
]

---

# App Outputs

.pull-left[
### Map outputs

- Glacier extent comparison
- Historical-to-recent retreat map
- Susceptibility distribution
- Temperature and terrain support
]

.pull-right[
### Analytical outputs

- Glacier area change
- Temperature change
- Warming trend
- Local retreat susceptibility
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# Methodology

## Data integration and analytical workflow

---

# Methodology Overview

.panelset[
.panel[.panel-name[Step 1: Glacier Mapping]
- Landsat 7, 8 and 9 summer imagery is used to map glacier extent
- NDSI supports historical and recent glacier inventories
- Retreat is defined as ice present historically but absent in the recent composite
]

.panel[.panel-name[Step 2: Predictors]
- ArcticDEM provides elevation and slope
- Landsat surface temperature provides baseline temperature and warming trend
- Mining, settlement, and coastline distance rasters are added
]

.panel[.panel-name[Step 3: Susceptibility Model]
- Cleaned retreat and stable ice labels are used for training
- Random Forest is validated with spatial blocks
- Outputs are exported as a precomputed Earth Engine asset
]
]

---

# Key Datasets

| Theme | Main data source | Role in the app |
|------|------|------|
| Glacier mapping | Landsat 7 / 8 / 9 C2 | NDSI glacier extent and retreat detection |
| Glacier reference | GLIMS 2023 | Glacier boundary support |
| Topography | ArcticDEM v4 | Elevation and slope predictors |
| Climate | Landsat surface temperature | Summer temperature baseline and warming trend |
| Boundary and coast | LSIB Simple | Greenland boundary and coastline |
| Mining | Drillholes and licences | Anthropogenic proximity indicators |
| Human geography | Settlements and cities | Settlement screening context |

---

# Glacier Mapping Strategy

.pull-left[
### Boundary logic

- Historical glacier extent is built from summer composites around 1999–2002
- Recent glacier extent is built from summer composites around 2022–2024
- Retreat is mapped as historical ice lost in the recent period
]

.pull-right[
### Preprocessing

- Connected-pixel patch cleaning reduces noisy classification
- Seasonal snow and cloud contamination are filtered
- All layers are standardized to a 300 m polar grid
]

---

# Industrial and Exposure Layers

.pull-left[
### Anthropogenic footprint

- Mining drillholes
- Mineral licence areas
- Settlements and cities
]

.pull-right[
### Exposure screening

- Distance to mine features
- Distance to settlements
- Distance to coastline
- Overlap with susceptible glacier areas
]

---

# Terrain and Climate Support

.pull-left[
### ArcticDEM

- Elevation
- Slope
- Terrain controls near glacier fronts
- Greenland-wide standardized raster support
]

.pull-right[
### Thermal layers

- Early summer temperature baseline
- Recent summer temperature
- Pixel-level warming trend
- Landsat ST used as climate predictor
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# How It Works

## From user input to hotspot output

---

# App Logic

.panelset[
.panel[.panel-name[1–2]
.pull-left[
### 1. Select comparison period

The app loads glacier conditions for historical and recent periods.
]

.pull-right[
### 2. Display analytical layers

Users compare glacier extent, retreat, susceptibility, and predictor layers.
]
]

.panel[.panel-name[3–4]
.pull-left[
### 3. Click a location

The system calculates local statistics in a 25 km buffer.
]

.pull-right[
### 4. Interpret the result

Users assess whether glacier susceptibility overlaps with warming and mining-related proximity.
]
]

.panel[.panel-name[5]
### 5. Return outputs

- Hotspot maps
- Buffer summaries
- Spatial screening indicators
- Model diagnostics
]
]

---

# Example User Question

.pull-left[
### User asks

Which glacier areas in Greenland appear most susceptible to future retreat and are also close to mining activity or settlements?
]

.pull-right[
### App returns

- Susceptibility surface
- Retreat history context
- Nearby anthropogenic features
- Local glacier-climate statistics
]

---

# Random Forest Design

.pull-left[
### Training labels

- Cleaned historical retreat pixels
- Stable glacier persistence pixels
- Ambiguous pixels excluded
]

.pull-right[
### Validation strategy

- 10 spatial folds across Greenland
- Folds 0–6 for training
- Folds 7–9 for testing
- Metrics include Accuracy, Kappa, Precision, Recall and F1
]

---

# Precomputed Asset

.pull-left[
### Why precompute?

- Landsat compositing and distance rasters are expensive at Greenland scale
- Random Forest modelling increases startup cost
- Precomputation improves app responsiveness
]

.pull-right[
### Asset stores

- Glacier extent 2000
- Glacier extent 2024
- Retreat mask
- Warming rates
- Temperature surfaces
- Susceptibility predictions
- Elevation and metadata
]

---

# Interactive Regional Statistics

.pull-left[
### Click-based analysis

For each selected location, the app calculates a 25 km buffer summary.
]

.pull-right[
### Returned statistics

- Glacier area change
- Temperature change
- Warming trend
- Mean elevation
- Local susceptibility
]

---

# Example GEE Logic

```js
var asset = ee.Image('projects/your-project/assets/greenland_susceptibility');
var glac2000 = asset.select('glacier_2000');
var glac2024 = asset.select('glacier_2024');
var retreat = asset.select('retreat');
var susc = asset.select('susceptibility');

Map.addLayer(susc, {min: 0, max: 1, palette: ['blue', 'yellow', 'red']}, 'Susceptibility');
```

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# Limitations

## What the platform can and cannot do

---

# Main Limitations

.pull-left[
### Technical

- Temporal mismatch exists across Landsat, ArcticDEM, and project assets
- Resolution mismatch affects comparison between predictors
- Greenland-wide processing remains computationally demanding
]

.pull-right[
### Interpretation

- Proximity does not prove causality
- Mining-related predictors indicate accessibility and exposure, not direct impact
- The app supports screening, not definitive attribution
]

---

# Future Improvements

.pull-left[
### Data and workflow

- Add stronger elevation-change datasets
- Improve temporal consistency across predictors
- Expand mining and infrastructure inputs
]

.pull-right[
### Advanced analysis

- Improve model tuning and validation
- Add richer model interpretation outputs
- Extend toward planning and governance communication
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# References

## Key literature and datasets

---

# Literature and Data Sources

.pull-left[
### Key literature

- Greenland peripheral glacier retreat studies
- Arctic warming and cryosphere change studies
- Spatial machine learning and validation studies
]

.pull-right[
### Core datasets

- Landsat Collection 2
- GLIMS glacier outlines
- ArcticDEM Mosaic V4
- LSIB Simple boundary
- Mining and settlement assets
]

---

class: center, middle, inverse

# Thank You
